feedback.text: I had a game called 'On Rush' purchased and digitally downloaded from the PlayStation store. The game is an online multiplayer car racing game. This game had been on my PlayStation 5 console library ready to play. I hadn't played the game for a while as I was spending my time with other projects. When I decided I wanted to play On Rush. I could not find it on my PlayStation 5 console. I thought that was very strange as I hadn't removed the game myself. So I went onto the PlayStation store to re-download this game, however it was no where to be found. I searched on line looking for answers and I had found that the developers had stopped supporting this game and shut down the servers, preventing anyone from playing it. So Sony had removed this game 'On Rush' from the store, worst still, Sony deleted my copy from my PlayStation 5. (Im sure that illegal anyway?) You can buy a physical copy on this game on disc but apparently it won't work as its an online game only. I feel its injustice to take a product away from consumers that have give them money for it. Its the equivalent of buying a car from the manufacturer and then a year later, that manufacturer stealing the wheels from your car to prevent you from driving your car. It must be stopped as its the right thing to do. The EU knows the difference between right and wrong, correct... I wonder?
